items.name: Ops ES/MS Student Scheduler (GSheet Prep or App?)
items.creator: Jabari

notes.purpose: We should be mindful of when we're using the right tool for the job. Hyperscale LLMs like Claude are deceptively simple. They're heavy machinery but have the appearance of a simple tool. Perhaps a locally deployed model is better suited for something like a chatbot. There's reason to believe that costs are artificially low because AI companies are not accountable for all of their resource consumption (electric, water). Therefore, we should strive to make prompts thorough and reduce round trips (i.e token usage). https://claude.ai/code/session_01M5qRBFEauSWmRNYTfgymGY https://claude.ai/share/992c2135-bc38-4170-baca-230893b4e2b8

items.name: Next Generation Seat Tracker + Renewal App
items.creator: Cristina

notes.purpose: The Seat Tracker is used by several teams to: Communicate staffing models for the year (Finance → School Leadership) Assign teammates to available seats (School Leadership) Track currently and potentially open seats to plan recruitment efforts (School Leadership → Talent Acquisition) Track progress to staffing open seats (Talent Acquisition) Track Renewal decisions and port data to Renewal Approval Tool (School Leadership → Talent Acquisition/Employee Relations) The data flows to a Tableau workbook where Talent Acquisition team can communicate hiring progress and track their goals over time. Data also flows to Renewal Approval Tool to create renewal offers and approval routing of teammates in positions for the following school year. Eliminate $10/user/month fee for use of AppSheet ($12,000 annually), which has been plagued with sync issues and lack of support and development from Google.
notes.audience: Talent Acquisition, School Leadership, Finance, Employee Relations
nextSteps: Feature: All-in-one data entry and dashboard (no more switching to Tableau for data viz)
Feature: Integrate with Renewal approval process (no more switching to another tool to audit salary/title changes and create renewal letters)
Next Steps: Connect with Talent Acquisition team to discuss AppSheet limitations/pain points and gauge wish list for next iteration of the app.

notes.purpose: Replace current system of all different URLs for Tableau tools and Google sheets with one megasite for data access and visualizations created and version controlled in code using whatever tool is best for the job (Python charting libraries, Streamlit, Javascript) Offer new self service data tools pulling pre-defined metrics from Semantic Layer Offer new LLM "talk to the data" functionality to every dataset
notes.timeline: Rolled out gradually as we build semantic layer over 2026-2027
nextSteps: Next steps: Use Claude to supercharge semantic layer development (suggesting star schema design based on current reporting views, creating new dimensional and fact tables, creating yaml files for metadata and metric definitions).
Feature: Replace Tableau with self-hosted visualization and analysis website that reads from the semantic layer directly (no more individual calculations spread amongst various tools)
Feature: Ability to limit access to internal users but also create publicly available websites for outside users and the Board
productionAudit: Semantic Layer Product Selection Happening < 1 week
Need Auth + Permissions (including approved Job Groupings to stamp global access rules)

notes.purpose: Dramatically speed up process of converting wide reporting tables to star schema (facts and dims) by having Claude analyze the reporting views and suggest and create new facts and dims. Proposed workflow: CB2 prompts Claude by extract/rpt view, creates pull request for new fcts and dims Relevant analyst (+ Charlie if needed) review for accuracy since they know the model best. They can work in the branch to make the necessary changes, add descriptions to yaml, and push them once they are ready. Once in production, CB2 takes first pass at creating metrics in Cube. Analyst reviews metrics + finalizes
notes.timeline: Rolled out gradually as we build semantic layer over 2026-2027
nextSteps: Detailed Plan from Claude here: https://github.com/TEAMSchools/teamster/issues/3432
productionAudit: All pieces in place, just need to meet as a team to validate output and plan.
Investigate Cube API compatibility and see if we can write our yaml with schema and metadata directly to Cube and then use Cube UI to build out metrics only.

items.name: landing page for data team apps/tools on kippteamandfamily.org
items.creator: Kevin

notes.purpose: * Streamline the data entry process for operational walk-throughs and observations. * Automatically route and sync the collected observation data directly to Google Sheets.
notes.timeline: * When: Weekly, during standard observation protocols.
nextSteps: Evaluate the current Google Form process to determine if a custom replacement is necessary or beneficial.
Develop a dynamic, auto-updating list of staff members.
Implement ID matching to ensure staff identities map correctly for downstream reporting and tracking.
productionAudit: * Secure backend database or directory integration to manage the dynamic staff list.
* Reliable API connection/automation (e.g., Google Apps Script, Zapier, or custom backend) to push data to Google Sheets.
* Front-end UI/UX development for the entry form (if replacing Google Forms entirely).
* Data validation testing to ensure downstream tracking and ID matching work flawlessly.

notes.purpose: * Enable quick generation of test prep quizzes. * Utilize 900+ item bank for question sourcing. * Implement randomized question selection driven by grade-level filters.
notes.audience: * Who: Teaching & Learning (T&L) Math Assistant Directors (ADs), 3rd-8th grade Math Teachers, and Assistant Principals (APs).
notes.timeline: * When: Weekly, as part of assessment protocols.
nextSteps: Consolidate disparate tools/links into a single, unified application.
Develop QR codes or unique identifiers on the quizzes to enable future OCR scanning of student work.
Build batch processing capabilities for bulk quiz generation and delivery.
productionAudit: * optimize item, storage and access across multiple subjects and grades
* Backend logic and algorithms to handle accurate grade-level filtering and randomization without duplicates.
* A robust document generation engine (e.g., PDF rendering) capable of handling batch creation and formatting for bulk printing.
* Integration of a QR code/barcode generation library into the document rendering process.

notes.purpose: KIPP Forward team uses a Google sheet to track certain career prep milestones for alums and needed a solution that enabled roll-ups with updating student information from Salesforce (school attended, degree, expected graduation date). Originally going to replace Google sheet manual entry with an AppSheet. Used Claude to create a Next.js app that reads and writes to the database and has a nice clean UI to use.
nextSteps: Connect to OAuth for Google authentication
Create automation to add students with upcoming graduation dates to add to contact list.
Integrating data entry with analysis and visualizations

items.name: Focus SIS - Centralized Database of FLDOE Data Fields
items.creator: Laszlo

notes.purpose: * Centralize FLDOE student and staff data fields. * Eliminate the manual lookup of 600+ state PDFs.
notes.audience: * Who: Focus Implementation Team and Miami Director of Compliance.
nextSteps: Refine the PDF parser to improve data extraction accuracy.
Implement a monitor to detect FLDOE field changes automatically.
Automate data syncing directly from the state website.
Map state data fields to Focus SIS fields.
productionAudit: * Scalable PDF parsing infrastructure capable of handling complex state documents.
* Reliable web scraper or API monitor for detecting FLDOE website updates.
* Relational database and logic engine for state-to-SIS field translation.
* Automated serverless functions for data syncing and a compliance alert system.

notes.purpose: * Build a Claude powered project manager assistant * curate knowledge base to support the KIPP Miami Focus SIS transition. * Assist with strategic planning, communications, and stakeholder mapping.
nextSteps: Develop Claude plugins for other Focus team members.
Optimize knowledge base and context.
Integrate with Asana and Google Sheets for automated updates.
productionAudit: * Configure APIs and webhooks for Asana and Google Sheets integrations
